qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 开始执行全部 — execute all root-cause fixes for git hygiene across all repos. Verbatim from spec-20260520-221059 Section 5: 将你说的问题全部保存为新的spec
qa.overall_assessment: BA analysis is sound and implementable. All 5 validation dimensions checked. One spec_text_vs_execution_drift finding raised on M11 settings.json registration format (AC8 check is underspecified). One scope gap observation: repos not in BA scope (application-assistant, happy, knowledge-system, rednote-mcp) have .claude/ dirs but no actual harness output noise today — exclusion is currently justified but the new propagation hook will preventively cover them at next session. One factual note: docs/codex/ in dot-claude is already gitignored by docs/* at line 68, making the explicit docs/codex/ rule in M1 redundant but idempotent. All binding constraints are correctly encoded and enforced.

ba_validation_dimensions.detail: Every claim in the BA spec is backed by direct measurement evidence. git ls-files confirms asset-tracker.html tracked in budget-management (verified: 'asset-tracker.html' returned). git status confirms untracked docs/codex/ and tests/generated/ in multi-asset-portfolio and travel-planner. git status confirms case-39742/ untracked in orchestra. git ls-files confirms INDEX.md and README.md tracked in orchestra/docs/research/. Read of /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/.gitignore confirms last rule at line 90 with no tests/generated/, docs/codex/, or *.png rules. Read of /root/.claude.json confirms playwright args = ['--cdp-endpoint','http://127.0.0.1:8080'] with no --output-dir. All observations match BA's measured values.

User requirement 'execute all root-cause fixes for git hygiene across all repos' maps to 8 Must Have requirements (M1-M11) plus 1 Should Have. All repos with actual harness output noise are in scope. Repos with .claude/ but no current noise (application-assistant, happy, knowledge-system, rednote-mcp) are correctly excluded from the per-repo fix list — but the new M10 propagation hook will automatically correct them at next SessionStart. The Won't Have items are correctly scoped: removing !docs/dev/ and !docs/reference/ is explicitly forbidden. The docs/research/ blanket exclusion in orchestra is correctly won't-have. No universalist claim from the user ('all repos') is violated — BA correctly notes the propagation hook ensures future repos are covered without manual per-repo fixes.

universalist_claims_checked.ba_handling: M1-M11 covers all repos with current noise; propagation hook (M10) covers future repos
universalist_claims_checked.verdict: acceptable — no remaining repo has current untracked noise; hook ensures ongoing coverage

ba_validation_dimensions.detail: BA investigated each affected file directly: Read of each .gitignore file, git ls-files for asset-tracker.html, git status for repos with untracked content, git ls-files for orchestra docs/research/ tracked files, Read of /root/.claude.json. The root cause analysis identifies the auto-commit cron (commit 89771b3) as origin of asset-tracker.html tracking. BA correctly identifies that prior cycle 20260518-214050 was L1 (symptoms only) and this cycle is L3+L5 (data + infrastructure). Investigation is complete and deliverables are documented.

All 10 files listed in BA's files_to_modify/files_to_create were verified against the filesystem. Paths exist and contain the gaps BA claims. One nuance: BA lists /root/budget-management/.gitignore for BOTH M2 (*.html rule) and M6 (tests/generated/ rule) — this is correct, dev will make both changes to the same file. The component_chain entries reference accurate line ranges and evidence types.

objections.location: ticket-dev-20260525-050935.md § Technical Hints M11 + AC8 check definition
objections.description: The M11 Technical Hint shows appending a flat { 'type': 'command', 'command': '...', 'timeout': 10 } object to the SessionStart array. However, the actual settings.json SessionStart structure is an array of objects each with a 'hooks' sub-array: [ { 'hooks': [ { 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }, ... ] } ]. Every existing SessionStart entry wraps commands inside { 'hooks': [...] }. A flat top-level entry would be a non-conformant structure that may or may not be processed correctly by the harness. AC8's check only verifies 'session-gitignore-propagate.sh' appears somewhere in settings.json SessionStart — it does not verify the entry is structurally correct (wrapped in hooks sub-array). This means dev could add a structurally malformed entry and AC8 would still pass.
objections.evidence: python3 settings.json parse confirms: SessionStart = [ { 'hooks': [ {type,command}, ... ] } ]. All 6 existing entries use the { 'hooks': [...] } wrapper. BA hint shows flat { 'type': 'command', ... } at top level.
objections.proposed_fix: Update M11 Technical Hint to show: append { 'hooks': [{ 'type': 'command', 'command': 'bash "$HOME/.claude/hooks/session-gitignore-propagate.sh"', 'timeout': 10 }] } as a new element of the SessionStart array, OR append the inner { 'type': 'command', ... } object to SessionStart[0].hooks sub-array. Update AC8 check to explicitly require the hooks-wrapped format: check that the new entry is either (a) a new { 'hooks': [{ 'command': '...session-gitignore-propagate.sh...' }] } element in the SessionStart array, or (b) the command appears in SessionStart[0].hooks[]. The current AC8 check is underspecified and would pass with a non-conformant flat entry.
objections.rationale_for_non_blocking: The functional intent is clear (add the hook command to SessionStart). Dev is likely to use the correct wrapper format by analogy with existing entries. The risk is a structural malformation that may not execute — this is a clarity gap, not a logic gap. BA should update the hint and AC8 check before dev implementation, but it does not block approval given the low risk of misinterpretation.

constraint_1_docs_dev_preserved.evidence: dot-claude .gitignore line 69: !docs/dev/ (Read confirmed). BA spec BINDING CONSTRAINT 1 explicitly forbids removal. AC1 check requires !docs/dev/ present AND git check-ignore docs/dev/ticket.md exits 1. Won't Have list explicitly includes 'Removing !docs/dev/ from dot-claude .gitignore'.

AC1 check requires !docs/reference/ present. Won't Have list explicitly includes 'Removing !docs/reference/ from dot-claude .gitignore'.

constraint_3_orchestra_index_readme_trac.evidence: git ls-files confirmed: docs/research/INDEX.md and docs/research/README.md returned (both tracked). BA uses docs/research/case-*/ pattern (NOT docs/research/) specifically to avoid affecting these files. AC6 check explicitly verifies git ls-files docs/research/INDEX.md and README.md return non-empty. Won't Have list includes 'Adding docs/research/ blanket rule to orchestra'.

git_rm_cached_verification.evidence: M2 Must Have explicitly states: 'add root-level *.html rule to .gitignore AND run git rm --cached asset-tracker.html'. Technical Hint M2 repeats: '(a) add *.html rule to .gitignore; (b) run git rm --cached asset-tracker.html'. AC2 check verifies 'git ls-files returns empty for asset-tracker.html'. The git rm --cached requirement is fully documented.

s1_output_dashboards.detail: Should Have S1 explicitly addresses output/dashboards/asset-tracker.html and spending-dashboard.html. Verified: git ls-files output/dashboards/ returns both files as tracked. BA correctly notes these are inside the already-ignored output/ subtree — new files won't be tracked after .gitignore add, but existing tracked files need explicit git rm --cached. S1 is correctly classified as Should Have.
s1_output_dashboards.qa_verified: git ls-files /root/budget-management output/dashboards/ confirmed: output/dashboards/asset-tracker.html and output/dashboards/spending-dashboard.html both tracked

orchestra_pattern_safety.evidence: Pattern docs/research/case-*/ matches only subdirectories starting with 'case-'. Verified against current state: git status shows '?? docs/research/case-39742/' as untracked. git ls-files confirms INDEX.md and README.md are tracked and will not be affected by case-*/ glob. The glob does not match flat files at the docs/research/ level. Edge Case #6 in BA spec correctly explains: 'Does NOT match case.md or case-notes.md; INDEX.md and README.md at docs/research/ level remain tracked.'
orchestra_pattern_safety.risk: none — pattern is specific and correctly scoped

harness_propagation_hook_architecture.analysis: The proposed hooks/session-gitignore-propagate.sh is architecturally appropriate: (1) SessionStart lifecycle is correct — runs at session begin, not per-tool; (2) Idempotency via grep-before-append per rule is correct and safe; (3) Exit 0 on non-git-repo and on no-.claude/ dir is correct (prevents hook failures from blocking sessions); (4) Uses GIT_TOPLEVEL detection via git rev-parse, which is the correct approach; (5) The HARNESS_RULES array includes .claude/dev-registry/dev-*/ and .playwright-mcp/ in addition to tests/generated/ and docs/codex/ — this provides more comprehensive coverage than the per-repo M3-M8 fixes alone; (6) The hook is separate from the existing session_start.sh (session_start.sh vs session-gitignore-propagate.sh), which is correct per the spec's requirement.
harness_propagation_hook_architecture.m11_format_issue: See spec_text_vs_execution_drift OBJ-1 — M11 hint shows incorrect flat format vs actual settings.json schema requiring { 'hooks': [...] } wrapper. This is a clarity issue, not an architectural issue.

docs_codex_redundancy_note.detail: In dot-claude, docs/codex/ is already gitignored by the docs/* rule at line 68 (confirmed: git check-ignore -v docs/codex/foo.json returns '.gitignore:68:docs/*'). The explicit docs/codex/ rule in M1 is redundant but harmless — BA's Edge Case #1 explicitly acknowledges this and correctly advises dev to verify with git check-ignore before and after. The explicit rule is justified for clarity and future-proofing. This is not a finding requiring BA correction.

scope_gap_check.evidence: None of the excluded repos have current untracked tests/generated/ or docs/codex/ content (git status checked for application-assistant, happy, knowledge-system, rednote-mcp — all returned no matches). These repos are not generating harness output noise today. The M10 propagation hook will automatically add missing rules to any repo on next SessionStart, so explicit per-repo fixes are not needed for currently-clean repos. The scope boundary is correctly drawn.

out_of_scope_observation.observation: application-assistant, happy, knowledge-system, rednote-mcp all have .claude/ dirs and are git repos but lack tests/generated/ rule in .gitignore. They will be auto-fixed by the M10 propagation hook at next session. No immediate fix needed as they have no current noise.

all_findings.location: ticket-dev-20260525-050935.md § Technical Hints M11 + AC8 check
all_findings.issue: M11 hint shows flat { 'type': 'command', ... } format for settings.json SessionStart entry, but actual settings.json uses { 'hooks': [{ 'type': 'command', ... }] } wrapper for every existing entry. AC8 check verifies only that 'session-gitignore-propagate.sh' appears in the SessionStart section — it does not verify the correct { 'hooks': [...] } wrapper structure. A structurally malformed flat entry would pass AC8 but may not be executed by the harness.
all_findings.recommendation: Update M11 hint to use correct wrapper format: { 'hooks': [{ 'type': 'command', 'command': 'bash "$HOME/.claude/hooks/session-gitignore-propagate.sh"', 'timeout': 10 }] }. Update AC8 check to verify the command appears in either a hooks-wrapped new entry or in SessionStart[0].hooks sub-array.
